title: Tensions rise between US and Iran with threats to block the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The United States launches new attacks against Iran while the Revolutionary Guard threatens to close the Strait of Hormuz, raising tension in the Middle East and activating regional diplomatic efforts.

title: England defeats Norway in extra time and advances to the World Cup semifinals
summary: With two goals from Jude Bellingham, England comes from behind against Norway and secures its place among the top four in the tournament amid controversy over a disallowed goal.

title: A 33-year-old man arrested for murdering his partner with hammer blows in a Sapporo apartment
summary: Police arrest the suspect, who confessed to hitting the woman on the head with a hammer, causing her death in the apartment they shared.

title: Argentina defeats Switzerland and advances in the World Cup with a Messi corner kick goal
summary: The Argentine national team takes the lead against Switzerland thanks to a set piece by Lionel Messi from the corner, taking the lead on the scoreboard.

title: Rintaro Sasaki not selected on first day of Major League Baseball draft
summary: The Japanese prospect, son of a high school baseball legend, did not hear his name in the first four rounds of the MLB draft.

title: Prime Minister Takashi maintains official schedule with cabinet approval over 65%
summary: The government continues its regular activities, including an event in memory of Shinzo Abe, while polls show solid support for the administration.

title: Europe consolidates its World Cup hegemony with Spain, England and other powers in semifinals
summary: Three of the four semifinalists are European, confirming the tournament's trend, while the continent's royal families throw their support behind their teams.

title: World Cup generates debates on national identity, cultural barriers, and influence of manga
summary: Analysis articles reflect on the meaning of representing Japan, restrictions on foreign fans in the US, and how 'Captain Tsubasa' inspired a generation.

title: Japan promotes international cooperation in artificial intelligence while robotics and AI generate social debate
summary: The government accelerates global alliances to secure AI supply chains, in a context where technology permeates entertainment, security, and daily life, prompting cultural reflections.

title: Over 15% of doctors in hospitals exceed 960 annual overtime hours
summary: Despite government regulations, overwork remains a critical problem in the Japanese healthcare system, affecting doctors' quality of life.

title: Renunciations of inheritances reach historic record in Japan due to increase in unwanted properties
summary: Over 324,000 families renounced inheritances in 2025 to avoid tax burdens and declining properties, while the government plans a census of abandoned and unregistered properties.

title: Trump's White House accused of intimidating press by subpoenaing New York Times journalists
summary: The White House Correspondents' Association condemns the subpoena of NYT reporters investigating security flaws on Air Force One as an act of coercion against press freedom.

title: Japan receives record number of foreign tourists but 70% of accommodations suffer staff shortages
summary: The tourism boom in cities like Onomichi, driven by social media, contrasts with the severe labor shortage in the hotel sector, affecting the capacity to serve visitors.

title: Yoshinobu Yamamoto selected to All-Star Game for second straight year but will not pitch
summary: The Los Angeles Dodgers pitcher was selected for the MLB All-Star Game, but the team decided he will not participate in the game to protect his arm before an upcoming start.

title: Yui Ueda conquers Golden Slam in wheelchair tennis by winning Wimbledon without dropping a set
summary: The Japanese tennis player achieves a historic milestone by defeating her opponent 6-0, 6-0, culminating a perfect season that takes her to the top of world wheelchair tennis.

title: Japan successfully tests vertical landing of reusable rocket in space milestone
summary: JAXA and the private sector manage to have an experimental rocket take off and land vertically, paving the way for future reusable missions and reducing costs in space exploration.

title: Germany and Norway share educational strategies facing challenges of immigration and digitalization
summary: While Germany innovates to integrate immigrant children into classrooms, Norway warns Japan about mistakes to avoid in the educational digitalization process.

title: Ceramist Jun Isezaki and pianist Masako Muroi, pillars of Japanese culture, pass away
summary: The Japanese art world mourns the loss of the Bizen pottery master, designated a Living National Treasure, and the centenarian pianist who remained active until 105 years old.

title: Aomori Nebuta and Kyoto Gion festivals captivate with renewed exhibits and traditions
summary: The Nebuta festival museum reopens with a large 23-meter figure, while Kyoto prepares for the Gion Matsuri with special edition tenuguis reflecting pop and traditional culture.

title: China strengthens its presence in East Asian seas and strengthens ties with North Korea
summary: On the tenth anniversary of the South China Sea arbitration ruling, China intensifies its claims and military movements in the area, while strengthening diplomatic relations with Kim Jong-un's regime.
